commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
As a spider. As frail and weak as a spider's web; and miserable withal, whilst, like a spider, we spend our bowels in weaving webs to catch flies. (Challoner)
Meditabuntur is here used in a passive sense. (Tirinus)
Hebrew, "we have spent our years like one speaking a word. "(St. Jerome)
When he has done, the sound is no more: so their memory has perished with a sound, Psalm ix. 8. (Haydock)
Hebrew, Chaldean, do not mention the spider: Yet it seems to have been originally in the text, as it is recognized by the Syriac, Septuagint, and the other Greek interpreters, and makes the sense more complete. (Calmet)
The transcribers might more easily omit than the Septuagint, could insert this word. (Berthier)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
